content_body:
Fall in Blue Ridge, GA is a special sight to behold with the vibrant colors, brisk mountain air, and cozy evenings by the fire. Our Blue Ridge luxury cabin rentals boast the prime spot to take in all the fall colors right from your own private porch. 

When adventure and exploration call your name, a plethora of iconic fall activities await to bring joy to friends and family. Apple festivals, wine tastings, horseback riding, and hiking the Appalachian Trail are just a few popular fall activities to relish the vibrant colors.

Blue Ridge, GA Luxury Cabins with Fall Foliage Views

Above the Timberline

Nothing says fall like escaping to a Blue Ridge cabin rental decked out with all the amenities needed to add that special touch to your vacation. Ranging from 2-bedrooms to 5-bedrooms, our luxury cabins with mountain views, river access, or wooded views have just what you need to make the most of your Blue Ridge getaway!

Leaf Peeping in Blue Ridge, GA

Arguably the most beautiful time of year in Blue Ridge, autumn is prime time to explore some of the excellent hiking trails in North Georgia. Visit Amicalola Falls in all its glory, revel in the 360° views from Brasstown Bald (the tallest mountain in Georgia), or take a leisurely stroll around the Blue Ridge Lake Loop.

For the motorcyclists, take a scenic drive through the area to experience the fall foliage in style as you wind you way through the North GA mountains. Want to ride in style? Check out Epic Slingshot Rentals to take a spin and sling your way through Blue Ridge!

If mountain biking is more your speed, check out the many biking trails in the Blue Ridge area. Enjoy a scenic workout as you steer your way through the orange, red, and yellow peppered forests.

Private Fishing Trips In Blue Ridge, GA 

Known as the Trout Capital of Georgia, Blue Ridge is home to numerous fishing guides to help you catch the trout of a lifetime. From the Toccoa River to Noontootla Creek, rainbow trout and browns are abundant in the waters of Blue Ridge. No matter your skillset, Blue Ridge is sure to exceed your expectations this fall season when it comes to fishing.

Horseback Riding ~ Fall Trail Rides

Blue Ridge has been rated as one of the 50 most beautiful places in the world, and what better way to explore the gorgeous scenery than on horseback? We have the perfect terrain and weather for a beautiful ride in the Blue Ridge Mountains.

Wineries in North Georgia

If there's one experience that defines fall in Blue Ridge, it's wine tasting! Visit the vineyards that are sprinkled all throughout the North Georgia Mountains and sip on a local wine while taking in the gorgeous views. Roo Mountain Vineyards and Bear Claw Vineyards are just a few of our favorites. If a craft beer is more your taste, never fear! Several Blue Ridge breweries are waiting to offer you their fresh brews.

Family-Friendly Fall Activities in Blue Ridge, GA 

North Georgia offers the perfect location for a getaway full of family fun. After many years of tried and true research, we have collected the best activities for your fall vacation.

Enjoy a corn maze, pumpkin patch, hay rides, and more at Burt's Pumpkin Farm! This family farm has been a fan favorite for many years. What says fall more than this?

Ride the Blue Ridge Scenic Railway along the Toccoa River into the twin towns of McCaysville, GA, and Copperhill, TN. Stand on the state line and experience shopping, dining, and more before the train takes you back to Blue Ridge.

A staple of the Blue Ridge area, Mercier Orchards offers apple picking in the fall. Savor one of their famous fried apple pies, enjoy their homemade fudge, and sip on their fresh cider while there!

No fall getaway would be complete without exploring the deep lore of the Appalachian Mountains. Expedition: Bigfoot Museum offers a wide collection of Bigfoot-related items, and the experience is fun for the whole family.

Blue Ridge, GA Fall Festivals

Fall in Blue Ridge is defined by the many festivals that occur between September and November. In October, Arts in the Park features a wide array of local artists that proudly showcase their work.

The most popular fall festival is the Georgia Apple Festival in nearby Ellijay, GA. One of the largest fall festivals, there are over 300 vendors that share their crafts, foods, and more. Apples from local orchards in Ellijay, Georgia, are the center of this festival, with lots of homemade treats ranging from fried apple pies to making your own apple-scented candle. This festival occurs during the two middle weekends of October.

Book Your Fall Getaway Today

The Rivers Edge

Due to the high demand for luxury cabins in the fall, do not wait to reserve the perfect cabin for your fall getaway! Browse our selection or call our reservation specialists at (706) 432-2140 to reserve the best Blue Ridge vacation rental that suits your needs.

title: Fall Things To Do in Blue Ridge, GA
